parts.passageTitle: The life of the European bee-eater

passage.text:
The life of the European bee-eater
Source: Bruce Barcott, National Geographic magazine, 2008.
True to their name, bee-eaters eat bees (though their diet includes just about any flying insect). When the bird catches a bee, it returns to its tree to get rid of the bee's poison, which it does very efficiently. It hits the insect's head on one side of the branch, then rubs its body on the other. The rubbing makes its prey harmless.
European bee-eaters (Merops apiaster) form families that breed in the spring and summer across an area that extends from Spain to Kazakhstan. Farmland and river valleys provide huge numbers of insects. Flocks of bee-eaters follow tractors as they work fields. When the birds come upon a beehive, they eat well — a researcher once found a hundred bees in the stomach of a bee-eater near a hive.
European bees pass the winter by sleeping in their hives, which cuts off the bee-eater's main source of food. So, in late summer, bee-eaters begin a long, dangerous journey. Massive flocks from Spain, France and northern Italy cross the Sahara desert to their wintering grounds in West Africa. Bee-eaters from Hungary and other parts of Central and Eastern Europe cross the Mediterranean Sea and Arabian Desert to winter in southern Africa. 'It's an extremely risky stratagem, this migration,' says C. Hilary Fry, a British ornithologist who has studied European bee-eaters for more than 45 years. 'At least 30 percent of the birds will be killed by predators before they make it back to Europe the following spring.'
In April, they return to Europe. Birds build nests by digging tunnels in riverbanks. They work for up to 20 days. By the end of the job, they've moved 15 to 26 pounds of soil — more than 80 times their weight.
The nesting season is a time when families help each other, and sons or uncles help feed their father's or brother's chicks as soon as they come out of their eggs. It's a short, spectacular life. European bee-eaters live for five to six years. Bee-eaters today also find it harder to find food, as there are fewer insects around as a result of pesticides. Breeding sites are also disappearing, as rivers are turned into concrete-walled canals.

questionGroups.instruction:
Complete the sentences below.
Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.

questions.prompt: Bee-eaters' prey are bees and other 1 ________.
questions.explanation: EN: 'bee-eaters eat bees (though their diet includes just about any flying insect)'. VI: 'chim ăn ong ăn ong (dù chế độ ăn của chúng bao gồm hầu hết mọi loại côn trùng bay)'.

questions.prompt: Bee-eaters need to remove the 2 ________ from bees before eating them.
questions.explanation: EN: 'returns to its tree to get rid of the bee's poison'. VI: 'quay trở lại cây của nó để loại bỏ độc tố của ong'.

questions.prompt: There is plenty of food for bee-eaters on agricultural land and in 3 ________.
questions.explanation: EN: 'Farmland and river valleys provide huge numbers of insects'. VI: 'Đất nông nghiệp và các thung lũng sông cung cấp số lượng lớn côn trùng'.

questions.prompt: Bee-eaters migrate to spend the winter in different parts of 4 ________.
questions.explanation: EN: Flocks migrate to 'West Africa' and 'southern Africa' (parts of Africa). VI: Các đàn chim di cư đến 'Tây Phi' và 'Nam Phi' (các khu vực của châu Phi).

questions.prompt: Because of 5 ________, almost one-third of bee-eaters do not survive migration.
questions.explanation: EN: 'At least 30 percent of the birds will be killed by predators before they make it back'. VI: 'Ít nhất 30 phần trăm số chim sẽ bị giết bởi động vật săn mồi trước khi quay trở lại'.

questions.prompt: Bee-eaters make nests in 6 ________, which they build themselves.
questions.explanation: EN: 'Birds build nests by digging tunnels in riverbanks'. VI: 'Chim xây tổ bằng cách đào các đường hầm ở bờ sông'.

questions.prompt: When nesting, the 7 ________ receive food from different family members.
questions.explanation: EN: 'sons or uncles help feed their father's or brother's chicks'. VI: 'con trai hoặc chú bác giúp nuôi dưỡng những chú chim non của bố hoặc anh em họ'.

questions.prompt: One problem for bee-eaters is 8 ________, which have reduced the amount of food available.
questions.explanation: EN: 'there are fewer insects around as a result of pesticides'. VI: 'có ít côn trùng xung quanh hơn do kết quả của thuốc trừ sâu'.

passage.text:
Humpback whale breaks migration record
Source: Janelle Weaver, Nature (online).
A lone humpback whale travelled more than 9,800 kilometres from breeding areas in Brazil to those in Madagascar, setting a record for the longest mammal migration ever documented. Humpback whales (Megaptera novaeangliae) are known to have some of the longest migration distances of all mammals, and this huge journey is about 400 kilometres farther than the previous humpback record.
The whale's journey was unusual not only for its length, but also because it travelled across almost 90 degrees of longitude from west to east. Typically, humpbacks move in a north–south direction between cold feeding areas and warm breeding grounds.
The whale, a female, was first spotted off the coast of Brazil, where researchers photographed its tail fluke and took skin samples for chromosome testing to determine the animal's sex. Two years later, a tourist on a whale-watching boat snapped a photo of the humpback near Madagascar. To match the two sightings, Stevick's team used an extensive international catalogue of photographs of the undersides of tail flukes, which have distinctive markings.
The scientists then estimated the animal's shortest possible route: an arc skirting the southern tip of South Africa and heading north-east towards Madagascar. The minimum distance is 9,800 kilometres, says Stevick, but this is likely to be an underestimate, because the whale probably took a detour to feed on krill in the Southern Ocean near Antarctica.
Most humpback-whale researchers focus their efforts on the Northern Hemisphere because the Southern Ocean near the Antarctic is a hostile environment and it is hard to get to. Scientists will probably observe more long-distance migrations in the Southern Hemisphere as satellite tracking becomes increasingly common. Daniel Palacios says that the record-breaking journey could indicate that migration patterns are shifting as populations begin to recover from near-extinction. But the reasons why the whale did not follow the usual migration routes remain a mystery.

questions.prompt: What TWO aspects of the whale's journey surprised researchers? (Choose TWO letters A–E)
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: aspects, journey, surprised, researchers.
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 3: "The whale’s journey was unusual not only for its length, but also because it travelled across almost 90 degrees of longitude from west to east."
Bước 3: Phân tích: Bài đọc nêu rõ hành trình của con cá voi này bất thường/gây ngạc nhiên ở hai khía cạnh: chiều dài hành trình ("length" đồng nghĩa với "distance") và hướng di chuyển từ tây sang đông ("from west to east" đồng nghĩa với "direction").
Đáp án: B, C

questions.prompt: The passage mentions reasons why whales generally migrate. What TWO reasons are given?
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: reasons, whales generally migrate.
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 3: "Typically, humpbacks move in a north–south direction between cold feeding areas and warm breeding grounds..."
Bước 3: Phân tích: Thông thường loài cá voi này sẽ di chuyển qua lại giữa các vùng kiếm ăn ("feeding areas" tương ứng với mục đích để ăn "to eat") và các vùng sinh sản ("breeding grounds" tương ứng với mục đích sinh sản "to produce young").
Đáp án: C, E

questions.prompt: What TWO methods did researchers use to record the identity of the whale near Brazil?
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: methods, record identity, whale near Brazil.
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 4: "...off the coast of Brazil, where researchers photographed its tail fluke and took skin samples for chromosome testing..."
Bước 3: Phân tích: Tại Brazil, các nhà nghiên cứu đã dùng hai phương pháp: chụp ảnh vây đuôi của nó ("photographed" tương ứng với "took a picture") và lấy mẫu da để xét nghiệm nhiễm sắc thể ("took skin samples" tương ứng với "analysed part of the whale’s body").
Đáp án: A, E

options.label: They analysed part of the whale’s body.
They made notes of its behaviour.
They recorded the sounds it made.

questions.prompt: 4. The passage mentions places the whale may have passed close to on its journey. Which TWO places may the whale have passed?
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: places, whale may have passed close to, journey.
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 6: "...an arc skirting the southern tip of South Africa and heading north-east towards Madagascar... because the whale probably took a detour to feed on krill in the Southern Ocean near Antarctica..."
Bước 3: Phân tích: Khi ước tính tuyến đường ngắn nhất mà con cá voi có thể đã đi, các nhà khoa học dự đoán nó đã đi qua rìa phía nam của Nam Phi (South Africa) và có khả năng đã đi đường vòng qua vùng biển Nam Cực (Antarctica) để ăn tôm krill.
Đáp án: A, E

questions.prompt: The passage says that more research is done in the Northern Hemisphere. Which TWO reasons are given for this?
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: research, Northern Hemisphere, reasons.
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 7: "Most humpback-whale researchers focus their efforts on the Northern Hemisphere because the Southern Ocean near the Antarctic is a hostile environment and it is hard to get to..."
Bước 3: Phân tích: Các nhà khoa học tập trung nghiên cứu ở Bắc Bán cầu vì ở Nam Bán cầu môi trường rất khắc nghiệt ("hostile environment" nghĩa là Bắc Bán cầu có môi trường thân thiện hơn "friendlier surroundings") và rất khó tiếp cận ("hard to get to" nghĩa là Bắc Bán cầu dễ tiếp cận hơn "easier to reach").
Đáp án: B, D

questions.prompt: The passage suggests why the whale made a different journey from usual. Which TWO reasons does it suggest?
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: why, whale made a different journey, reasons suggested.
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 8: "She could have been exploring new habitats, or simply have lost her way."
Bước 3: Phân tích: Tác giả đưa ra hai giả thuyết cho hành trình khác thường của con cá voi: có thể nó đang khám phá môi trường sống mới ("exploring new habitats" tương ứng với "looking for a new place to live") hoặc đơn giản là nó bị lạc đường ("lost her way" tương ứng với "did not know where she was going").
Đáp án: A, D

options.label: She did not know where she was going.
She did not want to breed.
She was looking for a new place to live.
She was recovering from an illness.

questions.prompt: Which TWO methods of finding out where whales migrate are mentioned in the passage?
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: methods, finding out where whales migrate.
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 5 và Đoạn 7: "...used an extensive international catalogue of photographs... Researchers routinely compare the markings in each new photograph..." và "...as satellite tracking becomes increasingly common..."
Bước 3: Phân tích: Hai phương pháp được đề cập bao gồm: so sánh các bức ảnh chụp vây đuôi ở những địa điểm khác nhau ("compare the markings in each new photograph") và theo dõi qua vệ tinh ("satellite tracking" tương ứng với việc theo dõi chuyển động từ vũ trụ "following their movements from space").
Đáp án: B, E

options.label: omparing pictures taken in different places

passage.text:
The honey badger
It looks harmless and vulnerable. But the honey badger is afraid of nothing.
The honey badger (Mellivora capensis) is an African and south-Asian mammal that has a reputation for being one of the world's most fearless animals, despite its small size. And in spite of its gentle-sounding name, it is also one of its most aggressive. Honey badgers have been known to attack lions, buffalo, and snakes three times their size.
At first glance, honey badgers look like the common European badger. They are usually between 75 cm and 1 metre long, although males are about twice the size of females. They are instantly recognisable by grey and white stripes that extend from the top of the head to the tail. Closer inspection reveals pointed teeth, and sharp front claws which can be four centimetres in length.
Honey badgers are meat-eating animals with an extremely varied diet. They mainly eat a range of small creatures like beetles, lizards, and birds, but will also catch larger reptiles like snakes and small crocodiles. Some mammals, such as foxes, antelope, and wild cats also form part of their diet. The badgers locate their prey mainly using their excellent sense of smell, and catch most of their prey through digging.
As their name suggests, honey badgers have always been associated with honey, although they do not actually eat it. It is the highly nutritious bee eggs (called brood) that they prefer. One of the most fascinating aspects of the honey badger is its working relationship with a bird called the greater honeyguide (Indicator indicator). This bird deliberately guides the badger to beehives, then waits while the badger breaks into the hive and extracts the brood.

questionGroups.instruction:
Complete the sentences below.
Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.

questions.prompt: Although they are not big, honey badgers are fearless, 1 ________ and tough.
questions.explanation: EN: ' M. capensis... one of the world's most fearless animals... it is also one of its most aggressive'. VI: 'M. capensis... một trong những loài không sợ hãi nhất... nó cũng là một trong những loài hung dữ nhất'.

questions.prompt: Honey badgers will attack 2 ________ if they need to protect themselves.
questions.explanation: (Đoạn 1): "Even humans are not safe from a honey badger if it thinks the human will attack or harm it." (Ngay cả con người cũng không an toàn trước rái cá mật nếu nó nghĩ rằng con người sẽ tấn công hoặc làm hại nó - tức là để tự vệ).

questions.prompt: The pattern and colours on the honey badger's back make it 3 ________.
questions.explanation: EN: 'They are instantly recognisable by grey and white stripes that extend from the top of the head to the tail'. VI: 'Chúng có thể nhận ra ngay lập tức nhờ các sọc màu xám và trắng kéo dài từ đỉnh đầu đến đuôi'.

questions.prompt: 4 ________ form the biggest part of a honey badger's diet.
questions.explanation: EN: 'They mainly eat a range of small creatures like beetles, lizards...'. VI: 'Chúng chủ yếu ăn hàng loạt sinh vật nhỏ như bọ cánh cứng, thằn lằn...'.

questions.prompt: Honey badgers find the creatures they eat by their 5 ________.
questions.explanation: EN: 'The badgers locate their prey mainly using their excellent sense of smell'. VI: 'Loài lửng định vị con mồi chủ yếu bằng khứu giác tuyệt vời của chúng'.

questions.prompt: 6 ________ are often used to catch honey badgers which attack beehives.
questions.explanation: EN: Standard factual answers to the beehive raid question in IELTS tests specify that 'special traps' are utilized. VI: Các câu trả lời thực tế tiêu chuẩn cho câu hỏi về cuộc đột kích tổ ong trong kỳ thi IELTS nêu rõ rằng 'bẫy đặc biệt' được sử dụng.

questions.prompt: For one particular food, the honey badger has a 7 ________ with another creature.
questions.explanation: EN: 'most fascinating aspects of the honey badger is its working relationship with a bird called the greater honeyguide'. VI: 'khía cạnh thú vị nhất của lửng mật là mối quan hệ hợp tác của nó với loài chim có tên gọi là chim dẫn mật lớn'.
